title: We Will Rock You School Edition
description:
We Will Rock You School Edition follows two young rebels, Galileo and Scaramouche, in a dystopian future where individuality and musical instruments are outlawed.

They join a resistance group called the Bohemians to defeat the tyrannical Globalsoft Corporation, recover legendary rock relics, and restore the freedom of rock ‘n’ roll!!
address: Gulbenkian, University of Kent, Canterbury, Kent, CT2 7NZ, UK

title: Illuminate – Next Generation Dance Studio
description:
Join the pupils of Next Generation Dance Studio for an enchanting showcase, ‘Illuminate’, where music, movement, and creativity blend into a dazzling world of light and colour.

An Unforgettable Show Full of Colour, Song, and Dance. Prepare to be captivated as young performers light up the stage, singing and dancing with infectious joy and heart.

Share in the delight as the under-fives charm you with heartwarming routines, sure to bring a smile to your face and perhaps a tear to your eye. Be astounded by the radiant talent displayed by all our students, from the tiniest stars to our seasoned dancers, each bringing their unique energy and passion to the performance.

This is an unmissable opportunity to be swept away by the colour of song and dance, sparkling costumes, and the happiness of children taking pride in what they love most. Leave the theatre truly illuminated — and take with you a memory that will shine for days to come.

Do not miss your chance to experience ‘Illuminate’: a celebration of talent, joy, and the next generation of stars!
address: Gulbenkian, University of Kent, Canterbury, Kent, CT2 7NZ, UK

title: Ralph Vaughan Williams’ A Sea Symphony
description:
Ralph Vaughan Williams’ A Sea Symphony performed by the East Bridge Chorale and Caritas Chamber Choir.

The first half of our Summer concert features the much loved orchestral work Hebrides Overture (“Fingal’s Cave”) by Felix Mendelssohn followed by Ralph Vaughan Williams’ Serenade to Music performed by Caritas Chamber Choir.

The second half features Ralph Vaughan Williams’ A Sea Symphony (Symphony No.1), a thrilling four-movement choral symphony for soprano, baritone, chorus and orchestra that captures the power, mystery, and beauty of the sea. It sets text from Walt Whitman’s poetry to explore the sea as a metaphor for life and eternity. It is a landmark work composed between 1903 and 1909, first performed in 1910, in which the choir plays a role equal to the orchestra.

Music Director: Benedict Preece

East Bridge Concert Orchestra, Alexandra Caldon

David Stout, Baritone; Rebecca Hardwick, Soprano
address: Gulbenkian, University of Kent, Canterbury, Kent, CT2 7NZ, UK

title: Cloud 9
description:
Cloud 9

Presented by The Canterbury Players

Cloud 9 is a landmark play about sexual politics in colonial Africa and modern-day Britain, in which all our assumptions about sex and gender are stunningly exploded.

Following the story of one family in both colonial Africa and London in the 1970’s, Cloud Nine is about relationships – between women and men, men and men, women and women. It is about sex, work, mothers, Africa, power, children, grandmothers, politics… and money!

Join the Canterbury Players as we present this incredible play at the Gulbenkian theatre!

Production details

Cloud 9

Thu 16 - Sat 18 July
address: Gulbenkian, University of Kent, Canterbury, Kent, CT2 7NZ, UK

title: Canterbury Primary: Aladdin Jr
description:
Canterbury Primary: Aladdin Jr

Our talented Year 6 pupils are delighted to present Aladdin JR., a magical adventure filled with laughter, music and unforgettable characters.

Join Aladdin, Jasmine and the Genie as they embark on a journey of friendship, courage and wishes come true. Packed with energy, creativity and fantastic performances, this special production showcases the hard work and enthusiasm of our young cast. We look forward to welcoming you to an enchanting evening.
address: Gulbenkian, University of Kent, Canterbury, Kent, CT2 7NZ, UK

title: Treeline
description:
Treeline is a unique European musical and cycling venture joining countries and communities together on a 2500-mile arboreal journey.

Devised by maverick composer Graham Fitkin, this concert tour focuses on trees and biodiversity and has been produced with communities in ten different countries. Creative work has been produced from communities along the route. Data and sounds from specific trees from the Carpathian Mountains to Epping Forest are woven into this extraordinary new musical work.

Join us as Graham previews his amazing and important Treeline project, ahead of the concert in November. Graham will play some excerpts from the piece, explain the technology and talks about how he records trees and uses their sounds within the music.

The event will include a Q&A with Graham.
address: Gulbenkian, University of Kent, Canterbury, Kent, CT2 7NZ, UK

title: Canterbury Shakespeare Festival 2026
description:
Canterbury Shakespeare Festival returns in 2026 with an array of amazing performances!

Macbeth
23rd – 26th July
Shakespeare’s classic tale, told in a traditional production. Witness the dramatic clash between Macbeth and his foe Macduff come to life as his ambitions are spurred on by conniving Lady Macbeth.

Chekhov’s Seagull
30th July - 1st August
A drama exploring the unfulfilled lives, romantic entanglements, and artistic conflicts of characters at a Russian country estate.

PufflingPants
28th - 29th July
4th - 12th August
Shakespeare for people who don’t like Shakespeare! PufflingPants delivers bite-sized chunks from Macbeth, Julius Caesar, Twelfth Night and The Merchant of Venice.

The City-Heiress by Aphra Behn
6th - 8th August
Young rake George Wilding tries to keep his rich uncle happy whilst dallying with three different women.

Tempest in a Teacup
30th - 31st July
1st - 2nd August
A shortened family-friendly version of The Tempest with audience interaction.

The Winter’s Tale
13th - 15th August
A new take on Shakespeare’s classic comedy.
address: Gulbenkian, University of Kent, Canterbury, Kent, CT2 7NZ, UK

title: KPop Princess Pop Dream Tour
description:
KPop Princess Pop Dream Tour

Step into a neon-soaked universe where Royal Princesses and Fearless Demon Hunters trade their crowns and katanas for microphones and centre-stage spotlights.

Watch Dazzling Princesses and Fierce Hunter Girls unite to form the world’s most powerful Girl Group. It’s a story of friendship, big dreams, and total MAIN CHARACTER ENERGY! 💖🔥

This is a truly unique production — a hybrid collaboration bringing your favourite princesses and K-Pop hunter girls together like never before singing tribute renditions of all the biggest kids craze songs of the now. Expect insane, high-energy stage lighting perfectly synced with the vocals and choreography for a full concert-theatre experience.

We are not like the other shows on the market – This is a 1 of 1 production – The perfect family concert experience. You can expect:

  • Princess Pop Setlist Tribute performances inspired by today’s biggest pop icons including Sabrina Carpenter, Olivia Rodrigo, Tate McRae, Taylor Swift, Olivia Dean — plus high-energy K-Pop vibes inspired by Huntrix & Saja Boys.
  • Concert-Scale Production Huge synchronised stage lighting, powerful sound, and arena-style concert moments — all scaled perfectly for a family theatre audience.
  • Interactive Dance Moments Choreography, crowd dance-offs, and call-and-response moments that make every child feel part of the show.
  • Immersive Storytelling & Games Live challenges and fun character interactions woven into the concert narrative.

The perfect first “concert” experience for young fans Produced by the team behind the UK touring Princess Concert series, this is a one-of-a-kind hybrid theatre production you won’t see anywhere else.
address: Gulbenkian, University of Kent, Canterbury, Kent, CT2 7NZ, UK

title: Dear Zoo
description:
Dear Zoo

Presented by NLP

Dear Zoo the timeless children’s classic book by Rod Campbell returns to the stage!

See our characters write to the Zoo. Who will be delivered? A cheeky monkey, a jumpy frog and of course a perfect puppy.

Help your children learn as the book leaps onto the stage and share a magical experience as the story unfolds with child-engaging puppets, music and lots of audience interaction.

Rod Campbell is the master of interactive storytelling and an expert in early learning for pre-schoolers. As a trusted household name, his books have stood the test of time and continue to be a staple addition to the family bookshelf and a popular choice for early years’ teachers. The creator of more than 200 books for children, Rod Campbell’s unique ability to be both fun and reassuring encourages children to discover and delight in the world around them.

Celebrating 35 years in 2017, the story of a child who writes to the zoo asking them to send a pet continues to delight each new generation of children as they lift the flaps in search of the perfect animal.

We would love to welcome your school to this production. If you would like to book a school visit, please click here and complete the form.

Member Discount Applies

Members get 40% off tickets for this event.
address: Gulbenkian, University of Kent, Canterbury, Kent, CT2 7NZ, UK
